title: Cleistanthus stipitatus
taxonomy: Cleistanthus stipitatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Cleistanthus. The family Phyllanthaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the stone plants. Cleistanthus stipitatus is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Cleistanthus stipitatus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

It has been used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. intro: Cleistanthus stipitatus, also known as the Himalayan Cleistanthus, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
